Title: Arabidopsis WAT1 is a vacuolar auxin transport facilitator required for auxin homoeostasis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant-specific WAT1/Plant Drug Metabolite Exporter family protein; predicted 10 transmembrane domains
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates plant development, vascular/fibre differentiation, plant architecture and growth responses
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Facilitates auxin export from the vacuole to the cytoplasm, contributes to intracellular auxin homeostasis, and is required for normal stem fibre secondary cell wall thickness
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant analysis (wat1); local auxin rescue in stems; isolated vacuole 14C-IAA transport assay; heterologous transport assays in yeast and Xenopus oocytes; GFP co-localization; LC-MS/HPLC metabolite analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed during seed germination, seedling development, and stem/fibre-associated tissues; induced by IAA
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed earlier than visible stem phenotype onset; induced by 1 µM IAA in seedlings; promoter:GUS active in aerial tissues and roots
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous auxin treatment (IAA for induction; local 2,4-D or NAA application for phenotypic rescue); NH4Cl plus omission of Mg-ATP in vacuole transport assays
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter (p35S::WAT1-GFP); native promoter ProWAT1 in ProWAT1:GUS
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: wat1 mutation causes reduced secondary cell wall thickness of stem interfascicular fibres and altered expression of auxin-responsive genes
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to manipulate intracellular auxin compartmentation and fibre differentiation/secondary wall formation, but likely requires caution because auxin homeostasis broadly affects development
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a vacuolar auxin transport facilitator rather than enzyme or TF; in heterologous systems plasma membrane localization yields net IAA uptake from medium, corresponding to vacuolar export toward cytosol in planta; vacuoles contain IAA, oxIAA, IAA-Glc and precursors
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WAT1 is the core experimentally characterized gene in the study and encodes the first described vacuolar auxin transporter in plants. 2. WAT1 localizes mainly to the tonoplast and mediates IAA export from the vacuole, likely coupled to proton movement. 3. Loss of WAT1 alters auxin-responsive gene expression and causes reduced fibre secondary wall thickness, which can be rescued by exogenous auxin application.
